children.name: zig-syntax-overview
children.description: Core Zig syntax covering const/var declarations, function declarations, struct definitions, and doc comment conventions. Must use when reading or writing Zig files.

children.name: zig-control-flow
children.description: Zig control flow patterns including if/else expressions, while with continue expressions, for loops with index capture, and exhaustive switch statements. Must use when writing Zig control structures.

children.name: zig-types-declarations
children.description: Zig type system covering primitives, arrays, slices, optionals, enums, and tagged unions. Must use when reading or writing Zig files.

children.name: zig-build-system
children.description: Zig build system fundamentals including build.zig structure, standard targets, optimization options, dependency management with build.zig.zon, and custom build logic. Must use when configuring Zig projects.

children.name: zig-comptime-basics
children.description: Zig compile-time execution and metaprogramming including generic types as functions, comptime parameters, inline loops, and reflection with @typeInfo. Must use when writing generic or metaprogrammed Zig code.

children.name: zig-awesome-ecosystem
children.description: Curated catalog of libraries, tools, learning resources, and community projects from the Zig ecosystem. Must use when discovering third-party Zig packages, frameworks, or development tools.

children.name: zig-error-handling
children.description: Zig error handling with explicit error sets, error union types (!), try/catch/if err patterns, and best practices for error propagation. Must use when reading or writing Zig files.

children.name: zig-libxev
children.description: Cross-platform event loop library for non-blocking I/O, timers, signals, and async operations in Zig with zero runtime allocations and a C-compatible API. Must use when building async networking, event-driven applications, or integrating Zig event loops across macOS, Linux, and WebAssembly.

children.name: zig-allocators
children.description: Zig memory allocation patterns including GeneralPurposeAllocator, ArenaAllocator, FixedBufferAllocator, and page_allocator. Must use when writing Zig code that allocates memory.

children.name: zig-resource-management
children.description: Zig resource cleanup patterns using defer and errdefer for guaranteed cleanup on block exit and error paths. Must use when writing Zig functions with multiple allocation or file operations.

children.name: zig-migration-0.15
children.description: Breaking changes and migration guide for Zig 0.15 including I/O overhaul, ArrayList changes, usingnamespace removal, format string changes, and std.builtin.Type field renames. Must use when updating code from pre-0.15 to 0.15+.

children.name: zig-migration-0.16
children.description: Breaking changes and migration guide for Zig 0.16 including I/O as interface, @Type builtins split, packed type restrictions, vector indexing rules, and @cImport migration to build.zig. Must use when updating code from 0.15 to 0.16+.

name: zig-philosophy
description: Core Zig philosophy and design goals including the Zen of Zig, explicit control flow, explicit memory allocation, comptime over macros, and explicit error handling. Must use when reasoning about Zig design decisions.

children.name: ziggzagg
children.description: Zig code sample: ziggzagg.

children.name: curl
children.description: compile with `zig build-exe zig-curl-test.zig --library curl --library c $(pkg-config --cflags libcurl)`

children.name: hello-world
children.description: Zig code sample: hello-world.

children.name: samples
children.description: basic samples

children.name: generic-type
children.description: Zig code sample: generic-type.

children.name: c-interop
children.description: build with `zig build-exe c-interop.zig -lc -lraylib`

children.name: windows-msgbox
children.description: Zig code sample: windows-msgbox.

children.name: memory-leak
children.description: Zig code sample: memory-leak.

children.name: zig-stdlib-patterns
children.description: Common Zig standard library patterns for printing, ArrayList usage, string formatting, hashing, and crypto operations. Must use when reading or writing Zig files that use std.

children.name: zig-std-module
children.description: The Zig standard library entry point (`std.zig`). Documents re-exported types, module imports, and the compile-time `Options` struct. Must use when navigating or understanding std library structure.

children.name: zig-documentation-with-zigdoc
children.description: CLI tool for browsing Zig standard library and third-party module documentation from the terminal. Must use when looking up symbol signatures, doc comments, or exploring APIs without leaving the editor.

children.name: zig-linting-with-ziglint
children.description: ziglint rule reference, configuration via .ziglint.zon, inline ignores, and CLI usage. Must use when enforcing style, catching common anti-patterns, or setting up CI linting for Zig projects.

children.name: zig-language-server
children.description: The official Language Server Protocol implementation for Zig, providing completions, hover, goto definition, formatting, and semantic highlighting. Must use when setting up IDE support or editor integration for Zig development.

children.name: sig-strict-zig
children.description: Capacity-first memory model layer on top of the Zig compiler. Drop-in replacement enforcing caller-owned buffers, bounded containers, and visible allocations via .sig strict-mode files.
